_comment: FAV_HOLD + 0.78 FLOOR + CHOP GATE (REAL MONEY, deployed 2026-06-29, user-authorized). Trade favorites in [buy_p, sell_p], hold to settle (stop_p=0). PHASE-2 (backtest_real.py reproduces real result to $0.09; placebo + OOS): (1) buy_p RAISED 0.76->0.78 -- the cheap sub-0.78 'favorites' are systematically OVERPRICED (~0.70 for 62% winners), the real losers; placebo p=0.003/0.005, OOS +. The >0.85 tail is actually the BEST part so we do NOT cap low (price-CAP rejected, p=1.0). (2) chop_min=0.00056 = CHOP GATE: skip a round unless the underlying moved |spot-strike|/strike >= 0.056% from round-open to entry (decision-honest, no look-ahead); flat rounds are ~50/50 where favorites are overpriced. placebo p=0.005, OOS +0.10 pnl/trade, win 88-90%. Needs per-coin spot (live.py now fetches the coin's own Binance symbol, not BTC-only); gate NO-OPs when spot/strike unavailable. (3) sell_p RAISED 0.85->0.90 (Phase 7, 2026-06-30): we were skipping ~36% of favorites priced >0.85; the [0.85,0.90) zone is +EV in BOTH paper AND real ledger (real win-price margin +0.048, same as the in-band zone), placebo p<=0.005, OOS holds; total$ +109.5->+120.1, fire 61%->76%. STOP at 0.90 -- the [0.90,0.93) zone is +EV on idealized paper but -0.0485 in REAL money (slippage/adverse-selection on thin >0.90 offers). This fixes the 'btc barely fires' issue (btc, least volatile, has the most >0.85 strong favorites). CAVEAT: 8-day non-stationary window. Backups on box: portfolio.json.bak-pre078, portfolio.json.bak-pre-sellp90, live.py.bak-percoin.
